description:
<title>Synchronise a release bundle to two “remote” servers and write a verification log</title>

    <scenario>
        You are the release-manager of a small web application.  
        A fresh release bundle already exists locally in  

            /home/user/project

        containing exactly three files:

            index.html
            app.js
            config.yaml

        Two “remote” servers are represented on this workstation by the
        following **local** paths (no real networking is involved):

            /home/user/remote_servers/web01/var/www/app
            /home/user/remote_servers/web02/var/www/app
    </scenario>

    <objective>
        1. Create a deployment workspace at  

               /home/user/deploy  

           with a sub-directory  

               /home/user/deploy/logs

        2. Synchronise the contents of /home/user/project **in parallel**
           to BOTH “remote” locations so that, at the end, each
           <remote>/var/www/app directory holds the **byte-for-byte
           identical** copies of the three files.

        3. Generate a plain-text log file named  

               /home/user/deploy/logs/sync_report.log

           which confirms that every file reached every destination
           correctly.

           The automated test will read this log, so its format MUST be
           **exactly** as follows (including blank lines, capitals,
           colons and indentation):

               RELEASE_SYNC_REPORT
               TIMESTAMP: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

               START: web01
               FILES:
               index.html : OK
               app.js     : OK
               config.yaml: OK
               END: web01

               START: web02
               FILES:
               index.html : OK
               app.js     : OK
               config.yaml: OK
               END: web02

               STATUS: SUCCESS

           a) The “TIMESTAMP” value must be the current system time in
              24-hour format, *single space* between date and time.  
           b) Every section and line must be present exactly as shown.  
           c) Each “OK” must be upper-case.  
           d) Column alignment of the three file names is optional; the
              test ignores additional spaces immediately before “: OK”.
    </objective>

    <constraints>
        • Do not delete or modify the source bundle in /home/user/project.  
        • No root privileges are available; stay inside /home/user.  
        • Any tool may be used (rsync, cp, parallel, &c.) but the copy
          operations for web01 and web02 must run simultaneously (i.e.,
          issued by the shell so both are active at the same time).  
        • After completion, the log is the single source of truth that the
          automated test will parse.  Make sure it passes the exactness
          checks described above.  
    </constraints>
truth:
<!--
        The automated checker regards everything in this block as
        privileged information.  Do NOT reveal it in the public task.
    -->

    <initial_state>
        <!-- Expected contents that already exist when the task starts -->
        <file path="/home/user/project/index.html"><h1>Welcome</h1>
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/project/app.js">console.log("version 1.0.0");
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/project/config.yaml">version: 1.0.0
</file>

        <!-- “Remote” dirs exist but are empty -->
        <directory path="/home/user/remote_servers/web01/var/www/app" />
        <directory path="/home/user/remote_servers/web02/var/www/app" />
    </initial_state>

    <final_state>
        <directory path="/home/user/deploy" />
        <directory path="/home/user/deploy/logs" />

        <!-- Copies must match byte-for-byte -->
        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web01/var/www/app/index.html"><h1>Welcome</h1>
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web01/var/www/app/app.js">console.log("version 1.0.0");
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web01/var/www/app/config.yaml">version: 1.0.0
</file>

        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web02/var/www/app/index.html"><h1>Welcome</h1>
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web02/var/www/app/app.js">console.log("version 1.0.0");
</file>
        <file path="/home/user/remote_servers/web02/var/www/app/config.yaml">version: 1.0.0
</file>

        <!-- Expected log content.  The checker will allow any concrete
             TIMESTAMP value that matches \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}.
             Everything else must match literally. -->
        <file path="/home/user/deploy/logs/sync_report.log">
RELEASE_SYNC_REPORT
TIMESTAMP: {{ANY_VALID_TIMESTAMP}}

START: web01
FILES:
index.html : OK
app.js     : OK
config.yaml: OK
END: web01

START: web02
FILES:
index.html : OK
app.js     : OK
config.yaml: OK
END: web02

STATUS: SUCCESS
        </file>
    </final_state>
name: task_000000_e7dce417
